title: podman commit is very slower with large container diff？
body: we create podman container for run llm， and download some models from huggingface， this result diff is very large， then we run ```podman commit --log-level debug -f oci xxx xxx:v1```, but it run slower and paused by follow codes: 1: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2e738b9b871f98930d34ca97bc0eb19a324193ea/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/image.go#L1065 2: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2e738b9b871f98930d34ca97bc0eb19a324193ea/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/image.go#L1110 and we found srcHasher take a lot of time，i wanna to kown , how can we improve commit performance? thanks in advantage

comments.body: The image format requires a digest of the contents of the layers, and that's generated at commit-time for layers that aren't inherited from base images.  The amount of processing required to determine what should be included in the layer is affected by which storage driver is being used, but without `podman info` output, I don't know if there's anything to suggest there.

@nalind reference to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2e738b9b871f98930d34ca97bc0eb19a324193ea/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/image.go#L1095

how can i reset i.compression parameter with podman commit? or in storage.conf?

If you can switch from using fuse-overlayfs to the kernel's overlay filesystem, reading the data should require less CPU.
Compression is usually not used unless the destination requires it, which shouldn't be the case when committing to local storage.

@nalind thanks for reply， and i found io.Copy can not set buf size manually，default buf size is 32k which could lead to slower copy for large diff ,  if podman commit  and podman push could pass buf size parameter，that's would be great

i am confused about for follow two code:
1: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2e738b9b871f98930d34ca97bc0eb19a324193ea/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/image.go#L1110

if first one exe io.Copy why second code used Copy too？does it instead of mv?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
